title: Add custom snippet to Trip Pilot AI website

requirements.summary: Add user-provided custom code snippet to the Trip Pilot AI website in the appropriate location
acceptanceCriteria: The snippet is embedded in the correct location in the frontend codebase
The site builds and renders without errors after the snippet is added
The snippet is visible/active as intended (e.g., script executes, ad unit renders, widget appears)

file_operations.description: Create a new React component to safely render the user-provided custom snippet. Include a placeholder comment instructing where to paste the actual snippet code. The component should handle both inline scripts and external script loading, and should be designed to work with various snippet types (analytics, widgets, ad units, etc.). Use React's dangerouslySetInnerHTML only if the snippet contains HTML markup, or useEffect for script injection if it's a JavaScript snippet.

Import and render the CustomSnippet component in an appropriate location within the app shell (likely near the root level or within the router outlet) to ensure the snippet is loaded on all pages. Position it after ThemeProvider but before or after the router outlet depending on whether the snippet should appear on every page or only certain routes.
